# NullPunkt Solar Inc. — nullpunkt.ca

> German Precision · Zero Emissions. A new Calgary company, backed by SMB Solartechnik GmbH — a German parent installing integrated PV, battery and HEMS systems since 2024.

## Pages

- [Home](/): Integrated solar engineered to a single point — Calgary and Southern Alberta.
- [Services](/services): Four service lines — residential, commercial & agri, developer partnerships, lifetime monitoring.
- [Solar Calculator](/calculator): Calgary-specific PV yield, payback math and incentive stack.
- [Launch Roadmap](/work): NullPunkt Solar's Calgary launch milestones and 2026–2031 expansion plan.
- [About](/about): Company, parent (SMB Solartechnik GmbH) and engineering standards.
- [Contact](/contact): Email, office address and founding-customer waitlist.
- [Blog](/blog): Engineering notes and Alberta-specific energy market commentary.

---

## Company Master Data

- Legal name: NullPunkt Solar Inc.
- Parent company: SMB Solartechnik GmbH (Germany)
- Founder & CEO: Tim Brunkel
- Office address: #2005 – 10th Avenue SW, Calgary, Alberta, Canada
- Email: hello@nullpunkt.ca
- Phone: not yet published
- Website: https://nullpunkt.ca
- Service area: Calgary and Southern Alberta (expanding to Red Deer, Lethbridge corridor and developer subdivisions from 2027; selective expansion into British Columbia and Saskatchewan from 2028–2031)
- Launch: Summer 2026 (founding customer program live now)

## Services (Home overview)

Eyebrow: What we build
Headline: Four service lines. One integrated system.

Note: Every installation is delivered with an Alberta-licensed master electrician partner and engineered to the same standards our parent company has applied across several hundred German installations. We only work with fully licensed, high-qualification trades — no anonymous subcontractor chains.

01 · Residential Integrated Systems (B2C)
8–15 kWp PV, 10–15 kWh battery storage, intelligent HEMS, plus heat-pump and EV-charger readiness — engineered as one coordinated system for Alberta homes.

02 · Commercial & Agricultural (B2B)
20–200 kWp PV with optional storage and energy management for businesses, farms and small commercial properties. Demand-charge optimisation and Agri-PV included.

03 · Developer Partnerships (B2B)
Turnkey pre-integrated solar built into new-construction subdivisions — a premium sales feature for builders, fully coordinated with the construction schedule.

04 · Service, Monitoring & Maintenance (Recurring)
Lifetime cloud-based monitoring dashboard, optional annual maintenance packages and premium-component warranty management for every installed system.

---

## Services Page (Detail)

Eyebrow: What we build
Headline: Four service lines. One integrated system.
Intro: NullPunkt Solar engineers PV, battery storage and intelligent energy management as a single coordinated installation — not four products bolted together later.

### 01 · Residential Integrated Systems — B2C · Calgary metro
Fully integrated solar energy systems for owner-occupied homes. Engineered as one ecosystem rather than a stack of separately installed devices — increasing self-consumption from 25–40% (PV-only) to 70–85%.
- 8–15 kWp Tier-1 PV with Hail Class HW5 modules
- 10–15 kWh LFP battery storage (UL 9540)
- Intelligent HEMS — OpenEMS-based, no vendor lock-in
- Heat-pump and EV-charger readiness pre-wired
Reference pricing: CAD $26,000 – $48,000

### 02 · Commercial & Agricultural — B2B · Businesses & farms
Larger-scale solar with optional storage and energy management for commercial buildings, farms and small-industrial sites. Demand-charge optimisation, peak shaving and Agri-PV configurations.
- 20–200 kWp PV with NA-certified inverters
- Optional battery storage for peak shaving
- Demand-charge and time-of-use optimisation
- Agri-PV configurations for farms
Reference pricing: CAD $65,000 – $220,000+

### 03 · Developer Partnerships — B2B · New construction
Volume turnkey installations for new-construction subdivisions, integrated directly into the homebuilding process. Premium integrated solar becomes a marketable feature for the builder.
- 8–10 kWp pre-integrated unit per home
- Volume pricing coordinated with build schedule
- Marketing collateral for builder sales teams
- Single point of contact for the entire subdivision
Reference pricing: CAD $22,000 – $28,000 per unit

### 04 · Service, Monitoring & Maintenance — Recurring · Lifetime
Recurring revenue programs that keep every installed system performing for decades. Real-time monitoring, scheduled inspections and premium-component warranty management.
- Lifetime cloud-based monitoring dashboard
- Optional annual maintenance subscription
- 40-year module warranty management (premium tier)
- 5-year NullPunkt Solar workmanship guarantee
Reference pricing: Included + optional packages

Closing CTA: Let's run the numbers for your address. Free consultation. Full yield simulation. Fixed-price proposal you can compare to anything else on the table. → Join the founding waitlist

---

## About (Home section)

Eyebrow: Why NullPunkt
Headline: A new name in Calgary. A proven German engineering playbook.

NullPunkt Solar Inc. is the wholly-owned Canadian subsidiary of SMB Solartechnik GmbH — an established German solar company with hundreds of completed integrated installations.

We bring the engineering playbook, the supplier network and the partner standards to Alberta — and we only work with fully licensed, high-qualification trades on the ground. Every installation is delivered together with an Alberta-licensed master electrician partner, so the first system we commission in Calgary is held to the same standard our parent applies to every install in Germany.

### The Point Zero promise
- Zero guesswork — Every proposal includes the full yield simulation, the bill of materials and the payback math — before you sign.
- Zero handoffs — One team designs the PV, sizes the battery, configures the HEMS and commissions the lot. No subcontractor chains.
- Zero surprises — Fixed-price quotes, climate-engineered hardware and warranties measured in decades — backed by a parent company built to last.

### Meet the founder — Tim Brunkel
The person behind every Calgary install.

Tim founded SMB Solartechnik in Germany in 2024 and scaled the parent company past CAD 1.2M in annual sales, personally overseeing several hundred integrated PV installations across residential, commercial and agricultural sites. Before solar he spent 13+ years leading IT innovation and product programs in senior roles at a major German industrial group.

He's relocating to Calgary as principal directing mind of NullPunkt Solar Inc., with significant committed capital and a hand on every early Alberta project. If you book a site visit in our first year, the engineer at your kitchen table is the founder.

### Stat strip
- Parent company: SMB Solartechnik
- Integrated installs: Hundreds, DE
- Calgary launch: Summer 2026
- Calgary sunshine: 2,396 hrs/yr

---

## About Page (Detail)

Eyebrow: About NullPunkt
Headline: A new Calgary company, with a proven German playbook already running.
Intro: "NullPunkt" is the point of origin on a technical drawing — coordinate zero, where every measurement starts. That's where we want your home's energy balance to sit: production matching consumption, exactly.

### Our origin — Spin-off, not start-up.
NullPunkt Solar Inc. is the wholly-owned Canadian subsidiary of SMB Solartechnik GmbH — an established German solar company with hundreds of completed integrated installations.

We didn't show up in Calgary to learn the trade. We came because the Alberta grid, the housing stock and the sun hours finally make the numbers work — and because we wanted to build here from day one with the engineering playbook already written.

### How we work — One team. Whole system.
Most installers sell what their supplier pushes. We design around your bills, your roof and your appetite for grid independence — then pick the modules, the inverter, the battery and the energy-management controller.

Engineering, procurement, installation and commissioning all sit under one roof. The person who designed your system is on site when it gets switched on.

### Meet the founder — Tim Brunkel, Founder & CEO

Hi — I'm Tim. I founded SMB Solartechnik GmbH in 2024, grew the parent company past CAD 1.2M in annual sales in Germany's hyper-competitive solar market, and personally oversaw several hundred integrated photovoltaic installations across residential, commercial and agricultural projects.

Before solar, I spent 13+ years in senior technology roles at a major German industrial group, leading cross-functional teams turning emerging technology into enterprise solutions. That product- and program-management background is exactly what fully integrated solar needs: PV + battery + heat pump + EV + intelligent energy management (HEMS / OpenEMS) is a software-meets-hardware problem, not a panel-on-a-roof problem.

I'm relocating from southern Germany to Calgary as principal directing mind and sole Director of NullPunkt Solar Inc., with significant committed capital (bank-verified) backing the Canadian launch. I lived and worked in Canada once before — 18 months of Work & Travel back in 2008–09 — and I've wanted to come back ever since. Now I get to build something here that actually matters for Alberta's energy future.

If you join the founding waitlist in our first year, the engineer at your kitchen table will be me.

Founder stats:
- 13+ yrs · tech / product leadership
- 100s · PV installs overseen
- C2 · English (CELPIP 11)

### Partners on the ground — We only work with fully licensed, high-qualification trades.

Lead electrical partner — German-Canadian, Alberta-licensed master electrician
Our planned default electrical partner for Calgary installations is a German-Canadian, Alberta-licensed master electrician with long-standing local experience and an Interprovincial trade endorsement. Working with vetted partner crews — rather than anonymous day labour — means every NullPunkt residential and commercial install runs to Canadian Electrical Code with German engineering discipline on both sides of the project. Partner details are confirmed in the proposal for each site.

Standards we hold ourselves to:
- Canadian Electrical Code + Alberta-licensed master electricians on every project.
- Tier-1 modules with IEC 61215 Class 3+ hail rating, UL 9540 storage.
- The same engineering, commissioning and documentation discipline our parent has applied across several hundred German installations.
- 5-year NullPunkt workmanship warranty stacked on top of manufacturer warranties.

### The Point Zero promise
- Zero guesswork — Full simulation, bill of materials and payback math before you sign anything.
- Zero handoffs — Same crew from first site visit to commissioning. No subcontractor chains.
- Zero surprises — Fixed-price quotes, hardware-backed warranties, and a service contract built to last.

Closing CTA: Want to be one of our first Calgary installs? Founding customers get priority engineering slots and a direct line to me and the team. → Book a free consultation

## Roadmap / Launch (Portfolio section + Work page)

Eyebrow: Launch roadmap
Headline: NullPunkt Solar, coming to Calgary.
Intro: We're a new operation in Alberta — but not a new company. Our German parent has been installing integrated systems for years. Get on the list while founding slots are open.

### Founding customer program · Summer 2026
Be one of the first 25 integrated systems in Alberta.

Founding customers receive priority engineering, fixed-price proposals locked in before commissioning, and a direct line to the team that designed the system.

- Full yield simulation against long-term Calgary irradiance data (NRCan)
- 10 kWp PV + 10 kWh storage + HEMS reference systems from CAD $26,000
- Solar Club retailer modelling — up to ~35 ¢/kWh export credit
- CEIP option: 0% PACE financing repaid through property tax
- Fixed-price proposal — not a back-of-envelope quote

CTA: Join the waitlist

### Milestones (Home)
- Now — Founding waitlist open. First 25 Calgary homes receive priority engineering slots and founding-customer pricing.
- Q3 2026 — Calgary HQ stand-up (in progress). Office lease finalised, local electrical partner contracts signed, supplier logistics with SMB Solartechnik mirrored to Canadian distributors, permitting playbook live.
- Summer 2026 — First Alberta installations. Residential integrated systems go live across Calgary metro — fully monitored from day one.
- 2027 → — Commercial & developer rollout. B2B partnerships activated in Red Deer, Lethbridge corridor and new-construction subdivisions.

### Roadmap page

Headline: Coming to Calgary, Summer 2026.
Intro: We're new to Alberta — not new to the work. NullPunkt Solar is the Canadian subsidiary of SMB Solartechnik GmbH, a profitable German solar company with hundreds of completed integrated installations. Be one of our first 25 Alberta projects.
CTA: Join the founding waitlist →

Launch roadmap:
- Q2 2026 — Pre-launch & permitting. Alberta incorporation, micro-generation paperwork, supplier logistics from SMB Solartechnik transferred to Calgary.
- Summer 2026 — First Calgary installations. Founding-customer residential systems commissioned across Calgary metro — fully monitored from day one.
- Late 2026 — First commercial & agri builds. 20–200 kWp commercial and agricultural installations come online across southern Alberta.
- 2027 — Regional expansion. Red Deer, Lethbridge corridor; developer subdivision rollouts begin.
- 2028 – 2031 — Western Canada. Selective expansion targeted into British Columbia and Saskatchewan, building toward a meaningful installed base of integrated systems by year five.

Stats:
- Integrated installs by parent (Germany): Hundreds
- Founding Calgary slots · Summer 2026: 25 homes

## Calculator Page

Eyebrow: Calgary · Alberta · Canada
Headline: How much sun is your roof wasting?
Intro: Calgary gets more solar than Berlin, Frankfurt, or Munich. Run the same engineering math our German parent company has used on hundreds of European installations — calibrated for Alberta's grid, weather, and tariffs.

### Calgary at a glance
- 333 sunny days / year — More than Miami. Calgary is one of Canada's top solar cities.
- 1,292 kWh / kWp / year — Specific yield — beats Berlin (~1,050) and most of Europe.
- +8% winter cold bonus — Cold panels are efficient panels. Alberta winters help.
- ~450 g CO₂ / kWh grid — Alberta's grid carbon intensity (AESO, 2024 after the last coal unit converted to natural gas); every solar kWh still meaningfully cleaner than the grid mix.

### The calculator — Engineering math, not marketing fluff.
Built on Calgary's real irradiance data, Alberta's all-in retail rate, and the Micro-Generation export credit — grounded on the same numbers our engineers use.

Inputs include monthly power bill, roof orientation, optional Solar Club retailer scenario and CEIP (0% PACE financing) toggle. The tool returns a recommended system size, PV + battery cost breakdown, side-by-side retailer comparison (Standard ~16.5 ¢/kWh consume, ~8.5 ¢/kWh export vs. Solar Club ~8.4 ¢/kWh consume, ~35 ¢/kWh export), annual savings, simple payback and a year-one cash-flow view when CEIP is enabled.

Disclaimer: Engineering baseline computed locally · Not a binding quote.

### Money on the table — Alberta & federal incentives, as of 2026
We don't sell the rebate — we engineer the system. But every NullPunkt proposal includes a stacked incentive breakdown so you see your real, after-rebate cost.

- Solar Club (UTILITYnet retailer program) — ~35 ¢/kWh export
  An Alberta-only retailer rate structure offered through UTILITYnet that pays roughly 35 ¢/kWh for exported solar and charges around 8.4 ¢/kWh for consumption (verified May 2026 — rates set monthly). It dramatically improves payback for systems that export a meaningful share of production. Not always the best fit — when self-consumption is very high (e.g. big battery + heat pump), a standard retailer can win. We model both in your proposal.
- Clean Energy Improvement Program (CEIP) — 0% PACE financing
  $0-upfront property-assessed clean energy financing for residential and commercial owners in 26+ participating Alberta municipalities (Calgary, Edmonton, Lethbridge, Banff and more). Repaid through the property tax bill over up to 20 years; obligation transfers with the property if sold (Alberta Municipalities).
- Clean Technology Investment Tax Credit — 30% refundable
  Federal refundable tax credit on the capital cost of new solar PV and battery storage for commercial, agricultural, and developer-scale projects in Canada. Available through Dec 31, 2034 (CRA / NRCan).
- Alberta Micro-Generation Regulation — Net billing
  Small micro-generators under 150 kW are credited monthly at their retail energy rate for surplus exported to the grid. Systems from 150 kW to 5 MW are settled hourly. Administered by the Alberta Utilities Commission.
- Agrivoltaics & cold-climate research funding — Project-based
  Federal NRCan and Alberta-administered programs (RDAR, ERA) co-fund agri-PV and cold-climate solar demonstrations. We help eligible farms and developers structure applications.
  The $40,000 interest-free federal loan closed to new applications on October 2, 2025. Existing approved loans are unaffected. We're tracking what replaces it federally and will flag any new residential program the moment it opens.

### Built for Alberta weather — Calgary breaks ordinary solar systems. Ours are over-engineered for it.

- Hail — Calgary sits in Canada's hail capital. We spec only IEC 61215 Class 3+ panels — the same panels rated for German alpine installations — and back them with our own workmanship warranty.
- Snow load & shedding — Alberta Building Code ground snow loads near Calgary reach 1.0 kPa. We design at 30°+ tilts with frameless or low-edge frames so snow sheds within hours of sunrise rather than sitting for weeks.
- Chinooks & thermal cycling — −25 °C to +15 °C in 12 hours is normal. We use UV-stabilised cabling, glass-glass modules where applicable, and torque-checked mounting — every joint engineered for ~80 K thermal swings.
- Deregulated electricity market — Alberta's energy-only market means rates swing 6–25 ¢/kWh. A correctly sized battery shifts your consumption to avoid peaks — turning volatility from a risk into a savings lever.

### FAQ — The questions Calgary homeowners actually ask.

Q: Is solar actually worth it in Calgary?
A: Yes — and the numbers are better than most Canadians assume. Calgary's solar yield (1,292 kWh/kWp) is on par with Northern Spain. Combined with Alberta's high all-in residential rate (~16.5 ¢/kWh), simple paybacks of 8–11 years are realistic for a properly engineered system.

Q: What size system do I need?
A: For an average Calgary single-family home (~8,500 kWh/year), 7–10 kWp covers most consumption. Add a battery if you want to ride through peak pricing or outages. Our calculator above gives you a properly-sized starting point in under a minute.

Q: How do hail and snow affect my system?
A: Our modules are hail-rated to 35 mm at 27 m/s (IEC 61215 Class 3). We mount at 30°+ tilts so snow sheds rapidly. Our German parent company has not had to replace a panel due to weather on its installations to date — and we engineer the Alberta systems to the same spec.

Q: Can I go fully off-grid in Alberta?
A: Possible for acreages and farms, but rarely cost-optimal. The Micro-Generation Regulation makes a grid-tied + battery system more economical for nearly every site. We'll model both scenarios honestly during your site visit.

Q: How long does installation take?
A: For a typical residential system: 1 day on the roof, 1 day for electrical + HEMS commissioning. Permitting and ENMAX/EPCOR interconnection adds 4–8 weeks depending on the utility's queue.

Q: Why German engineering for Alberta?
A: Germany installs over 1 GW of residential solar every month — they've already solved the integration problems (battery + EV + heat pump + grid) Canada is just starting to face. We bring that playbook to Calgary, with local crews and Canadian warranty coverage.

Closing CTA: Ready to talk to a real engineer? Founding slots for Summer 2026 are limited. Reserve yours — we'll prioritise your site visit when we open Calgary installations. → Join the founding waitlist
